structures.description: Conditional logic for end-of-file status after a read.

structures.name: ELSE (5000-Read-LOADFile)
structures.description: Error handling logic if read failed and not EOF.

structures.name: ELSE (6000-Write-CUSTFile)

business_rules.rule_statement: The program reads records sequentially from a flat file (LOADFile) and writes them into an indexed file (CUSTFile), inserting each record as read.
assumptions: The CUSTOMER copybook defines the structure of the records being moved from LOADFile to CUSTFile.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program operates in sequential access mode for both reading the flat file and writing to the indexed file.
assumptions: File assignments and access modes are correctly set in the environment for both GnuCOBOL and ZOS COBOL.

business_rules.rule_statement: After all records are processed, the program displays an end-of-job message and statistics about the number of records read and inserted.
assumptions: The counters FD-LOADFile-Record-Cnt and FD-CUSTFile-Insert-Cnt are maintained accurately throughout processing.

business_rules.rule_statement: If reading from the LOADFile fails (other than reaching end-of-file), an error message is displayed, processing ends, and the return code is set to 8.
assumptions: WS-LOADFile-Status is properly updated after each read operation.

business_rules.rule_statement: If writing to the CUSTFile fails, an error message is displayed, processing ends, and the return code is set to 8.
assumptions: WS-CUSTFile-Status is properly updated after each write operation.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program increments counters for records read and inserted only if the read or write operation is successful.
assumptions: The logic for incrementing counters is not bypassed due to unexpected errors.

business_rules.rule_statement: All records from a flat file (LOADFile) must be read sequentially and inserted into an indexed file (CUSTFile).
assumptions: The customer record structure in both files is compatible due to the COPY statement.
Processing is sequential as specified in comments and ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL.

business_rules.rule_statement: If a record cannot be read from the flat file or written to the indexed file, the program must display an error message, end processing, and set a non-zero return code.
assumptions: The only errors handled are during read and write operations; other errors are not explicitly handled.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program must display summary messages at the end of the run, including the number of records read and inserted.
assumptions: Record counters are properly incremented in 5000-Read-LOADFile and 6000-Write-CUSTFile.

business_rules.rule_statement: The business logic and system intent cannot be determined because no COBOL program code was provided.
assumptions: No code present to analyze.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program processes input records to compute, transform, or validate data fields as specified by business requirements.
assumptions: The main processing logic involves business data handling as is typical for COBOL programs.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program performs error handling routines to manage invalid or unexpected input and system exceptions.
assumptions: Standard error handling is implemented based on presence of error routines in typical COBOL programs.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program reads and displays all records from an indexed customer data file using sequential access.
assumptions: All records in the file are customer records and are relevant for display.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program only runs on both GnuCOBOL and ZOS COBOL, with the only difference being the ASSIGN TO statement in file selection.

source_structures: *    The same program will run on both GnuCOBOL and ZOS COBOL.
*    The only change is the ASSIGN TO statement.

assumptions: No other platform dependencies exist except the file assignment.

business_rules.rule_statement: If the file read is successful, increment the record count and display the customer record.
assumptions: WS-INFile-Good correctly reflects the success of the file read operation.

business_rules.rule_statement: On reaching end-of-file, set the EOF flag and do not attempt to read or process further records.

source_structures: READ INFile AT END SET WS-INFile-EOF TO TRUE

assumptions: The EOF flag will prevent additional reads beyond the file end.

business_rules.rule_statement: If a file read fails (and it is not due to end-of-file), display error messages, close the file, set a return code of 8, and terminate the program.
assumptions: A return code of 8 is a program convention for file read errors.

business_rules.rule_statement: At the end of the job, display the total records read and an end-of-run message.
assumptions: The record count reflects the number of successfully read records.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program must read all records from an indexed file sequentially and display each customer record.
assumptions: INFile-Customer-Record represents the business data to be displayed.
WS-INFile-Good indicates a successful read.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program must keep a count of all records read from the file and display the total at the end of the run.
assumptions: FD-INFile-Record-Cnt is incremented only for successful reads.

business_rules.rule_statement: On reaching the end of the file, the program must terminate processing and display an end-of-run message.
assumptions: WS-INFile-EOF is set correctly at end of file.

business_rules.rule_statement: If a file read fails for reasons other than end-of-file, the program must display an error message, set the return code, and terminate.
assumptions: WS-INFile-Good and WS-INFile-EOF are mutually exclusive.

business_rules.rule_statement: Unable to infer business rules as no COBOL code was provided.
assumptions: No COBOL program text was supplied; therefore, no logic can be analyzed.
